1 ^ ¿~Ut quid: 2 ^ Memor estō congregātiōnis tuae
Deus: quam possēdistī ab initiō~; redēmistī virgam hērēditātis tuae~; mons Siōnis in quō habitāvistī in eō. ^ Remember thy congregation
repulistī in fīnem: which thou hast possessed from the beginning~; the sceptre of thy inheritance which thou hast redeemed~; mount Sion in which thou hast dwelt.

1 ^ ¿~Ut quid: 3 ^ Levā manūs tuās in superbiās eōrum in fīnem
Deus: quanta malignātus est inimīcus in sanctō. ^ Lift up thy hands against their pride unto the end
repulistī in fīnem: how many things the enemy hath done wickedly in the sanctuary.

1 ^ ¿~Ut quid: 4 ^ Et glōriātī sunt quī ōdērunt tē in mediō sōlemnitātis tuae~; posuērunt signa sua
repulistī in fīnem: ^ And they that hate thee have made their boasts in the midst of thy solemnity~; they have set up their ensigns

1 ^ ¿~Ut quid: 5 ^ et nōn cognōvērunt sīcut in exitū super summum. ^ and they knew it not as in the going out upon the highest top.
6 ^ Quasi in silvā lignōrum secūribus
Deus: excīdērunt iānuās ejus in idipsum~; in secūrī et asciā dēiēcērunt eam. ^ As with axes in a wood of trees
repulistī in fīnem: they have cut down at once the gates thereof~; with axe and hatchet they have brought it down.

1 ^ ¿~Ut quid: 7 ^ Incendērunt ignī sanctuārium tuum~; in terrā polluērunt tabernāculum nōminis tuī. ^ They have set fire to thy sanctuary~; they have defiled the dwelling place of thy name on the earth.
8 ^ Dixērunt in corde suō cognātiō eōrum simul
Deus: «~Quiēscere faciāmus omnēs diēs fēstōs Deī ā terrā.~»^ They said in their heart
repulistī in fīnem: the whole kindred of them together
īrātus est furor tuus super ovēs pāscua: "Let us make to cease all the festival days of God from the land."

1 ^ ¿~Ut quid: 9 ^ Signa nostra nōn vīdimus~; jam nōn est profēta
Deus: et nōs nōn cognōscet amplius. ^ Our signs we have not seen~; there is now no prophet
repulistī in fīnem: and he will know us no more.

1 ^ ¿~Ut quid: 10 ^ ¿~Usquequō
repulistī in fīnem: improperābit inimīcus~? ¿~Inrītat adversārius nōmen tuum in fīnem~? ^ How long
O God: shall the enemy reproach~? Is the adversary to provoke thy name for ever~?

1 ^ ¿~Ut quid: 11 ^ ¿~Ut quid āvertis manum tuam
Deus: et dexteram tuam dē mediō sinū tuō in fīnem~? ^ Why dost thou turn away thy hand
repulistī in fīnem: and thy right hand out of the midst of thy bosom for ever~?

1 ^ ¿~Ut quid: 12 ^ Deus autem rēx noster ante saecula~; operātus est salūtem in mediō terrae. ^ But God is our king before ages~; he hath wrought salvation in the midst of the earth.
13 ^ Tū confirmāvistī in virtūte tuā mare~; contrībulāvistī capita dracōnum in aquīs. ^ Thou by thy strength didst make the sea firm~; thou didst crush the heads of the dragons in the waters.
14 ^ Tū confrēgistī capita dracōnis~; dedistī eum ēscam populīs Aetiōpum. ^ Thou hast broken the heads of the dragon~; thou hast given him to be meat for the people of the Ethiopians.
15 ^ Tū dīrūpistī fontēs et torrentēs~; tū siccāvistī fluviōs Ētae. ^ Thou hast broken up the fountains and the torrents~; thou hast dried up the rivers of Etam.
16 ^ Tuus est diēs
Deus: et tua est nox~; tū fabricātus es aurōram et sōlem. ^ Thine is the day
repulistī in fīnem: and thine is the night~; thou hast made the morning light and the sun.

1 ^ ¿~Ut quid: 17 ^ Tū fēcistī omnēs terminōs terrae~; aestātem et vēr
Deus: tū plasmāvistī ea. ^ Thou hast made all the borders of the earth~; the summer and the spring

1 ^ ¿~Ut quid: 18 ^ Memor estō hujus
repulistī in fīnem: et populus insapiens incitāvit nōmen tuum. ^ Remember this
īrātus est furor tuus super ovēs pāscua: the enemy hath reproached the Lord
O God: and a foolish people hath provoked thy name.

1 ^ ¿~Ut quid: 19 ^ Nē trādās bēstiīs animās confitentēs tibī
Deus: et animās pauperum tuōrum nē oblīvīscāris in fīnem. ^ Deliver not up to beasts the souls that confess to thee
repulistī in fīnem: and forget not to the end the souls of thy poor.

1 ^ ¿~Ut quid: 20 ^ Respice in testāmentum tuum
repulistī in fīnem: quī obscūrātī sunt terrae domibus inīquitātum. ^ Have regard to thy covenant
īrātus est furor tuus super ovēs pāscua: for they that are the obscure of the earth have been filled with dwellings of iniquity.

1 ^ ¿~Ut quid: 21 ^ Nē āvertātur humilis factus confūsus~; pauper et inops laudābunt nōmen tuum. ^ Let not the humble be turned away confounded~; the poor and needy shall praise thy name.
22 ^ Exsurge
Deus: Deus~; jūdicā causam tuam~; memor estō improperiōrum tuōrum
repulistī in fīnem: eōrum quae ab insapientī sunt tōtā diē. ^ Arise
īrātus est furor tuus super ovēs pāscua: O God~; judge thy own cause~; remember thy reproaches with which the foolish man hath reproached thee all the day.

1 ^ ¿~Ut quid: 23 ^ Nē oblīvīscāris vōcēs inimīcōrum tuōrum~; superbia eōrum quī tē ōdērunt ascendit semper. ^ Forget not the voices of thy enemies~; the pride of them that hate thee ascendeth continually.
